new_text: Option F (A+B1+B3): Rule 5b + Heuristic Auto-Tagging + Model Confidence Tagging. Score: 86, Net gain: +73. Model tags confidence (verified_now/historical/inferred) when saving. LanceDB adds confidence column. Search results show [state_type | confidence] pair. Learning ability: 78→85. Consistency: 90→92. Output quality: 90→92. Multi-agentic: 92→95. Net gain is LOWER than Option C (+113) because F loses on token cost (-40), speed (-18), memory (-48). Consider revisiting only if learning ability gap proves problematic in practice. Upgrade path: A → C → F additive.

Claude Code MCP has THREE config layers: (1) Global ~/.claude/mcp.json, (2) Project .mcp.json in repo root, (3) Local ~/.claude.json under projects."<path>".mcpServers (private, managed via `claude mcp add/remove -s local`). Local scope is default for `claude mcp add` without -s flag. Commands: `claude mcp list` (all servers), `claude mcp get <name>` (scope), `claude mcp remove "<name>" -s local`, `claude mcp add -s local <name> -- <cmd> <args>`.

Model Router REST API (bypass MCP): http://127.0.0.1:18800. Endpoints: POST /fan-out {prompt, n} → fan to N free models. POST /compare {prompt, models} → compare+score. POST /research {topic, n} → synthesize. POST /schedule {prompt, cron, n} → recurring. GET /models → list+health. Response: JSON array {name, text, latency_ms, error}. MCP bridge at ~/.claude/integrations/model-router/mcp_bridge.py has 180s timeout.

Whisper optimization (Feb 2026, Telegram bot, CPU-only, faster-whisper turbo). Current: turbo (809MB), beam_size=5, first call ~40s (load+transcribe), subsequent ~35s for 2-7s audio (model loaded but slow). CRITICAL: 35s is NOT model-load, it's beam_size=5+temperature fallback on short audio. Fix: set temperature=0, beam_size=1 → ~3-8s expected. Alternative: Groq API ($0.04/hr, 20 RPM free tier, 200-500ms latency for 5s clip). Distil-whisper: 6x faster, 50% smaller, English-only, ~1.5-3s for 5s audio on 8-core i7, WER within 1% of large-v3.

michaelshimeles/ralphy: Production-grade Ralph orchestrator (~2000+ lines bash/TypeScript). Multi-engine (7+ AI CLIs), parallel with git worktree isolation, brownfield support (.ralphy/config.yaml), retries (--max-retries N), GitHub integration (issues sync, auto-PR), task sources (Markdown/YAML/JSON/Issues), webhooks (Discord/Slack), cost tracking, sandbox (symlinks), per-task branching. vs ralphy (2000+ lines) + quickstart (50-line POC). We have gates (12), memory (312 ChromaDB), tests (985), teams, causal tracking, 21 skills. Ralph has multi-engine, GitHub sync, webhooks. Ralph economics: $10.42/hr on Sonnet 4.5.

coleam00/ralph-loop-quickstart (110 stars): Bash orchestrates fresh Claude instances per iteration. Loop: read PROMPT.md → find task from prd.md (JSON with passes:false) → implement → verify with agent-browser → log to activity.md → commit → exit. Detects <promise>COMPLETE</promise> signal. Components: ralph.sh, PROMPT.md, prd.md, activity.md, agent-browser-skill. NEW VALUE vs Megaman: (1) Browser Automation (Vercel agent-browser: open, snapshot, screenshot, click, fill), (2) Fresh context per iteration, (3) JSON task format, (4) Termination signal. Megaman better on gates (12), memory (ChromaDB 309), teams, causal tracking, tests (985).

MCP Skill Library vs Hook-Injected Skills (Session 363). MCP: active judgment, no false positives, ~50 tokens idle, built-in usage tracking. Hook-injected: automatic, false positive risk (keyword matching), adds latency to every message, 0 tokens idle. HYBRID OPTION (best): Hook detects keywords, injects one-line nudge ("[Skill hint: /research available]"), ~15 tokens per nudge, no false positive cost, MCP server as backend. DECISION: Built MCP approach. Hook layer could be added later as nudge-only.

Session 363: Built MCP Skill Library Server (skill_server.py ~130 lines). Registered: `claude mcp add --scope user -t stdio skills`. Tools: list_skills() → names+descriptions+token estimates. invoke_skill(name) → full SKILL.md, logs usage. skill_usage() → history+counts. System prompt skills (7, ~3,812 tokens): brainstorm, writing-plans, implement, test, review, commit, wrap-up. MCP library (12, 0 idle): explore, fix, deep-dive, status, research, learn, prp-wave, ralph, chain, deploy, web, build. Token savings: 12,236 → 3,812 = 8,424 tokens/message. All 19 skills available.
